---
title: "What's new"
subtitle: "Pages on this site, sorted by most recently modified."
description: "Sortable, filterable index of every page on the UIAO site with its last-modified date. Use this to find pages that have changed recently."
date-modified: last-modified
toc: false
listing:
  - id: site-pages
    contents:
      - "**/*.qmd"
      - "!_*.qmd"
      - "!whats-new.qmd"
      - "!about/index.qmd"
      - "!docs/adapters/**"
      - "!docs/architecture/**"
      - "!docs/ci/**"
      - "!docs/documents/**"
      - "!docs/meta/**"
      - "!docs/onboarding/**"
      - "!docs/patterns/**"
      - "!docs/scratch/**"
      - "!docs/appendix/README.qmd"
      - "!docs/user-guides/README.qmd"
      - "!findings/README.qmd"
    type: table
    sort: "date desc"
    fields: [date, title, subtitle, categories]
    field-display-names:
      date: "Modified"
      subtitle: "Subtitle"
      categories: "Tags"
    sort-ui: [date, title, categories]
    filter-ui: true
    page-size: 50
    date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD"
categories: [meta]
---

This page lists every page on the UIAO site, sorted by most recently
modified. Pages without an explicit `date:` field in frontmatter fall
back to the **git last-modified time** of their source file, so a
formatting-only commit will register as an update.

Use the **search box** to filter, click any **column header** to re-sort,
and subscribe to the **Atom feed** linked in the page header for
notifications.

::: {.callout-note}
## Where the dates come from
Pages with `date:` in frontmatter use that value. Otherwise Quarto falls
back to `git log` mtime. Frontmatter dates win — settle on a single
field convention if you want a clean signal.
:::

## All pages

:::{#site-pages}
:::
